overview.description: A native select component for selecting one option from a list.
overview.purpose: A native select component for selecting one option from a list.
whenToUse: When you need to present a list of options for the user to choose from.
whenNotToUse: For single input fields or when space is not a concern.

examples.basic: <mc-select-native label="Pick a number" fit="medium" options="[{"value":0,"label":"Zero"},{"value":1,"label":"One"}]" placeholder="Pick a number from a list" id="field" hint="" variant="default" width="auto" labelposition="top" name="digits" value="[{"value":"1","label":"One"}]" selectedindex="[1]" errormessage=""></mc-select-native>

Vue3.minimal:
<mc-select-native
  label="Pick a number"
  :options="[{value: null, label: 'Zero'}, {value: 1, label: 'One'}, {value: 2, label: 'Two'}, {value: 3, label: 'Three'}, {value: 4, label: 'Four'}, {value: 5, label: 'Five'}]"
  @change="yourEventHandler(e)">
</mc-select-native>
Vue3.basic:
<mc-select-native
  label="Pick a number"
  hiddenlabel
  fit="small"
  :options="[{value: null, label: 'Zero'}, {value: 1, label: 'One'}, {value: 2, label: 'Two'}, {value: 3, label: 'Three'}, {value: 4, label: 'Four'}, {value: 5, label: 'Five'}]"
  placeholder="Pick a number from a list"
  hint="value"
  variant="vanity"
  labelposition="left"
  name="digits"
  @change="yourEventHandler(e)"
  value="[object Object]"
  :selectedindex="[1]"
  invalid
  errormessage="value"
  disabled
  required>
</mc-select-native>

VanillaJs.minimal:
<script type="module"
  src="./node_modules/@maersk-global/mds-components-core-select-native/index.js"></script>

<mc-select-native
  label="Pick a number">
</mc-select-native>

<script>
  document.querySelector('mc-select-native').options=[{value: null, label: 'Zero'}, {value: 1, label: 'One'}, {value: 2, label: 'Two'}, {value: 3, label: 'Three'}, {value: 4, label: 'Four'}, {value: 5, label: 'Five'}];
  document.querySelector('mc-select-native').addEventListener('change', (e) => yourEventHandler(e));
</script>
VanillaJs.basic:
<script type="module"
  src="./node_modules/@maersk-global/mds-components-core-select-native/index.js"></script>

<mc-select-native
  label="Pick a number"
  hiddenlabel
  fit="small"
  placeholder="Pick a number from a list"
  hint="value"
  variant="vanity"
  labelposition="left"
  name="digits"
  value="[object Object]"
  invalid
  errormessage="value"
  disabled
  required>
</mc-select-native>

<script>
  document.querySelector('mc-select-native').options=[{value: null, label: 'Zero'}, {value: 1, label: 'One'}, {value: 2, label: 'Two'}, {value: 3, label: 'Three'}, {value: 4, label: 'Four'}, {value: 5, label: 'Five'}];
  document.querySelector('mc-select-native').addEventListener('change', (e) => yourEventHandler(e));
  document.querySelector('mc-select-native').selectedindex=[1];
</script>

props.name: label
props.description: Label can be passed as a simple argument like: `label="Label"` or as a slot to mc-select-native. Use argument style for passing short labels, use slot when you want to pass label with HTML text. Label is required attribute as it will be used as aria-label.

props.name: hiddenlabel
props.description: If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value, then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only

props.name: options
props.description: Options are required. They can be passed as an array: `["One", "Two"]` or as an array of objects: `[{value: 1, label: "One"}, {value: 2, label: "Two"}]`

props.name: placeholder
props.defaultValue: Pick a number from a list
props.description: The `placeholder` attribute specifies a short hint that describes the expected value of a field (e.g. a sample value or a short description of the expected format)

props.name: id
props.description: Id passed to the field and used with label.

props.name: hint
props.description: Hint can be passed as simple argument like: `hint="hint text"` or as a named slot: ```hint text as HTML``` Use argument style for passing short hints, use named slot when you want to pass hint with HTML text

props.name: width
props.description: The width of the inner input container in percentage. If not provided the width will be `auto`.

props.name: labelposition
props.description: Position of the label. On small screens, label will always appear on top. See examples under input component on how to set label with and align text within the label.

props.name: name
props.description: Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element

props.name: value
props.description: Specifies the selected options. If the `options` are supplied as an array of strings, then the `value` should follow the same pattern, i.e. `["One", "Two"]`. Otherwise if `options` are supplied as an array of objects the `value` should be i.e. `[{value: 1, label: "One"}, {value: 2, label: "Two"}]`.

props.name: selectedindex
props.description: Specifies the selected indexes. If supplied, then the value array will be disregarded.

props.name: invalid
props.description: If `true`, the border is red and the `errormessage` can be displayed if provided.

props.name: errormessage
props.description: Error message will be shown only if `invalid` attribute is set to `true`. Error message can be passed as simple argument like: `errormessage="error"` or as a named slot: ```error message as HTML``` Use argument style for passing short messages, use named slot when you want to pass error message with HTML text

events.name: change
events.description: Emitted when the selected status changes.

slots.description: Use the label named slot, when you want to pass the label as HTML. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: label

slots.description: Use the hint named slot, when you want to pass the hint as HTML. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: hint

slots.description: Use the errormessage named slot, when in invalid state, you want to pass the error message as HTML. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: errormessage

members.name: label
members.description:
Label has to be passed as a property.
It is required attribute as it will be used as `aria-label`.

members.name: labelposition
members.description: Position od the label and text alignment within label.

members.name: name
members.description: Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element.

members.name: value
members.description: The initial value of the input.

members.name: hiddenlabel
members.description:
If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value,
then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only

cssParts.description: Using this CSS part you can change the look of the label container. Check Examples -> Customised appearance story to see live example.
cssParts.name: label-container

cssParts.description: Using this CSS part you can change the look of the label. Check Examples -> Customised appearance story to see live example.
cssParts.name: label
